subject: xpad: Add 8BitDo Ultimate Controller ID (#36)
author: Ruud Klaver

upstream.vanilla_quote: { 0x2dc8, 0x3106, "8BitDo Ultimate Wireless / Pro 2 Wired Controller", 0, XTYPE_XBOX360 },

impact.effect_if_absent: 8BitDo Ultimate Wireless Controller (VID:PID 0x2dc8:0x3106) would not be recognized by xpad driver, but vanilla 6.18.38 already provides equivalent support
affected_hardware: 8BitDo Pro 2 Wired Controller (may share same USB ID)

notes: Fork commit (2022-12-21) added VID:PID 0x2dc8:0x3106 with description 'Ultimate Wireless Controller' and also added `XPAD_XBOX360_VENDOR(0x2dc8)` to the second (xpad_table) ID-match table (verified via `git show`: 2 files/1 file, 2 insertions across both tables). Vanilla upstream commit 21617de3b4647175a4c6a2a58f40515a94a13df4 (2023-01-27, later) added the same VID:PID with description 'Pro 2 Wired Controller' plus the matching `XPAD_XBOX360_VENDOR(0x2dc8)` entry. Vanilla 6.18.38 currently has both: the xpad_device[] entry (line 409) with a refined merged description 'Ultimate Wireless / Pro 2 Wired Controller', and XPAD_XBOX360_VENDOR(0x2dc8) plus XPAD_XBOXONE_VENDOR(0x2dc8) in xpad_table (lines 586-587, the latter added by unrelated later 8BitDo commits, not this one). Functionally equivalent: same VID:PID, same XTYPE_XBOX360, same 0 mapping flags — only the description string differs (cosmetic). Not carried in any patch file: grepped 0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch directly for '2dc8'/'3106'/'8BitDo' — the vanilla entry passes through unchanged (context line 150 of the patch shows it untouched), confirming vanilla provision is what our build actually uses. No Main_MiSTer coupling found (grepped for '2dc8'/'3106' — no hits). Provenance doc correctly identified upstream coverage via vanilla commit 21617de3b464. sonnet-verified: confirmed disposition/equivalence/device-ID/no-userspace-coupling and vanilla-SHA ancestry (`git merge-base --is-ancestor 21617de3b4647175a4c6a2a58f40515a94a13df4 v6.18.38` holds); corrected vanilla_file_line from a 2-line range (409-410) to the single matching line (409) — line 410 is a DIFFERENT, unrelated entry (0x2dc8:0x3109 'Ultimate Wireless Bluetooth') that the original quote did not actually cover; normalized the vanilla SHA to its full 40-character form.
